metadata.title: /context Check: Read Your Window Before It Fills
metadata.description_blurb: A grading session hit 78% context before the build started — treat the context window like a kitchen counter: check it, clear it between tasks, or move the heavy reading to a subagent.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B06: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: She ran /context mid-session. The bar showed 78% full. The grading tool she was building had used 30%. The other 48% was policy documents, LMS exports, and meeting notes she had pasted earlier to gather context. The build had not started. The window was already crowded. Quality degrades monotonically as the window fills. She had not checked the counter once. This is Liam, in for Bear.

The context window is a fixed working surface. When it fills, Claude begins dropping earlier content — including constraints, examples, and instructions stated before the crowding happened. Liu 2025 shows quality degrades monotonically with window length, even with perfect retrieval. The conversation is not progress. Code on disk is progress. Instructions in CLAUDE.md are progress. Chat is disposable infrastructure.
beats.new_visual_element: Why it degrades — fixed surface / constraints dropped / Liu 2025 / chat is disposable infrastructure

beats.narration_text: Three tools manage the window. /context: run it before any new task to see what is filling it. /clear: wipes the conversation; safe because durable rules live in CLAUDE.md, not the chat, and code on disk survives. /compact focus on X: summarizes conversation history down to a gist when the history still earns its place — mid-task, when the thread matters but the verbatim does not.
beats.new_visual_element: Three tools — /context see what fills / /clear wipe between tasks / /compact keep gist / CLAUDE.md survives /clear

beats.narration_text: The structural fix for heavy reading is a subagent. Instead of pasting 25 student submissions into the main session, spawn a reader subagent: it reads in its own isolated context window and returns a three-section summary. The main session sees three paragraphs, not 25 submissions. The main session window stays available for the build. Run /context first; subagent when the reading is large.
beats.new_visual_element: Subagent alternative — reader in own window / main session sees summary only / window stays for build

beats.narration_text: Treat the context window like a kitchen counter. Check what is on it before you start cooking. Clear it between unrelated tasks. Move the heavy prep to a separate workspace — the subagent — when the main surface needs to stay clear. The window is your most limited resource in a Claude Code session. /context is the one command that tells you exactly how much you have left.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — kitchen counter / /context before every task / clear between / subagent for heavy reads

beats.narration_text: Run /context before every new task this week. For large reading tasks, paste this subagent pattern.
beats.new_visual_element: HANDOFF — context check + subagent reader prompt

props.command: Spawn a subagent to read these documents and return a summary. The subagent's job: read [these files / these submissions], produce a three-section report: (1) key findings, (2) patterns or gaps, (3) items that need my attention. Return only the report — not the source text. I will use the report in this session for the build.

beats.narration_text: If this keeps the build session from starting at 78%, subscribe. Liam, in for Bear.
